dsl_rule.translation_notes: Bearish key reversal day pattern: (1) New high above previous high confirms uptrend continuation, (2) Reversal low below previous low, (3) Close below previous close completes the reversal, (4) Higher volatility condition captured via hvol_20. Exit triggered on close above the reversal candle's high, indicating pattern failure. Well-established trend is implicit in regime filter TRENDING_UP. Exit condition reasonably inferred as pattern invalidation when price recovers above the reversal bar.

backtest_rule.reason: Bearish key reversal day pattern: (1) New high above previous high confirms uptrend continuation, (2) Reversal low below previous low, (3) Close below previous close completes the reversal, (4) Higher volatility condition captured via hvol_20. Exit triggered on close above the reversal candle's high, indicating pattern failure. Well-established trend is implicit in regime filter TRENDING_UP. Exit condition reasonably inferred as pattern invalidation when price recovers above the reversal bar.

source_rule_text: Enter a short position when a bearish key reversal day forms: a new high in an uptrend followed by a reversal to a low below the previous low and close below the previous close, especially with higher volatility and in a well-established trend.
